title: chelidonium lactucoides
intro: Chelidonium lactucoides, also known as Greater Celandine, is a perennial herb native to Europe and Asia, belonging to the poppy family (Papaveraceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial effects, which help to soothe and protect the skin and mucous membranes. The health benefits of Chelidonium lactucoides include its ability to reduce swelling and pain, treat skin conditions such as acne and eczema, and support liver and digestive health. Chelidonium lactucoides can be prepared as a tincture, topical cream, or tea, and is also used in homeopathic remedies to promote overall well-being.
